Ava Chen: Exec weekly — short read (2 min). Toplines: Private GA timeline, EU retention policy change (small), incident follow-up, and two quick asks. Please ack or flag blockers. :sparkles:

Ava Chen: Private GA remains on track for next week. Current blockers: final SLA paragraph, KMS flag in onboarding, and one last autoscale test. Sales has 2 anchor EAP accounts. Goals: ship SLA + onboarding checkbox by Thu; confirm autoscale test pass by Fri.

Rafi Malik: SLA WIP: inserting latency SLOs and credit table. Will push draft to /docs/sla/dedicated by Wed EOD. One question: should we publish p95 targets per-model-class or a single default?

Ava Chen: @Rafi prefer per-model-class if feasible; fallback to single default with appendix. Call it out in draft.

Simone Park: On EU retention: legal recommended tightening defaults to 14d ephemeral KV for EU-origin requests to reduce regulatory footprint. Product supports opt-in to 90d archival. Draft policy uploaded: https://redwood.internal/policies/eu-retention-v5 :lock:

Owen Brooks: Heads-up: analytics team flagged that 14d default affects ML signal freshness for few customers. Proposal: keep 14d default, provide explicit analytics-export opt-in with archival pipeline. I'll coordinate with eng-ml.

incident-bot: Incident summary: hosted-api-us-east outage at 4241245000 — impact ~22m. Root cause: autoscaler backpressure + burst path. Mitigation deployed 4241247200. Postmortem link: https://redwood.internal/incidents/4241245000 :clipboard:

Elena Varga: Follow-ups from postmortem: 1) add synthetic burst test to infra CI, 2) tune autoscaler cooldown to reduce flip-flop. I'll submit PRs for both by Thu.

Ava Chen: Good. Ops asks: 1) @Elena add synthetic test + cooldown PRs, 2) @Rafi deliver SLA draft Wed, 3) @Owen propose analytics opt-in UX by Thu. Also add KMS gating checkbox to onboarding checklist.

Rafi Malik: :thumbsup: will do SLA draft Wed.

Elena Varga: :thumbsup: PRs scheduled.

Owen Brooks: :thumbsup: will share opt-in UX mock and event mapping tomorrow.

Ava Chen: Quick metrics callout: p95 latency improved 9% MoM; cost per token down 5% after batching changes. Still seeing p99 tail spikes in high-concurrency flows — targeting that for next sprint.

Ava Chen: Final: I'll host a 15m sync Fri 4p if any blockers. React with :white_check_mark: if you can meet the deadlines above or drop blockers here.

Rafi Malik: :white_check_mark:

Elena Varga: :white_check_mark:

Owen Brooks: :white_check_mark:

Ava Chen: Thanks — will post a short follow-up summary later today.

Ava Chen: Quick founders flash 1-min read. Main points: Private GA checklist, slight tweak to EU defaults, and a short incident note. Please ack or flag blockers. :wave:

Ava Chen: Private GA is on-track. Need final SLA paragraph, KMS onboarding checkbox, and confirmation on per-customer canary. Please confirm by EOD Fri.

Jonah Price: Ack. SLA paragraph incoming Thu morning. One ask: can we make canary_duration configurable per account? Customers requested it.

Elena Varga: We can add "canary_duration" in rollout config, but it needs an extra validation step in Dedicated autoscale tests. Estimate 1 sprint to fully validate.

Samir Patel: Legal: EU data default = 30d ephemeral KV. Longer retention must be opt-in and explicitly surfaced in onboarding. Also Dedicated regulated customers must enable customer-managed KMS before GA.

legal-bot: Uploaded draft: /policies/eu-retention-v4. Link: https://redwood.internal/docs/drafts/eu-retention-v4 :page_facing_up:

deploy-bot: Started canary: private-ga/dedicated-rollout at 4241140150 in eu-west-1 (3 nodes). Monitoring active.

Ava Chen: Ops asks: 1) @Elena, run a quick load test and report p50/p95 impact, 2) @Jonah, SLA text by Thu, 3) product/docs update onboarding with KMS checkbox by Tue.

Elena Varga: Will run the test and post results in #eng-runtime by 6pm today. If p50 > +15% we revert the per-customer canary change for this release.

Jonah Price: :thumbsup: will deliver SLA draft Thu.

Ava Chen: Thanks team. If anything blocks GA, ping me immediately. Enjoy the rest of your week. :sparkles:

Ava Chen: Exec weekly snapshot  quick read (2 min). Main topics: Private GA checklist, EU data-retention policy change, brief incident summary, and two small org asks. TL;DR: solid progress, a couple of low-effort items to close by Friday. :sparkles:

Ava Chen: Private GA status: infra and Dedicated pools are in green. Launch window still targetted for next Tue pending a final SLA paragraph and KMS onboarding checkbox. Sales has 3 EAP customers lined up. Ops ask: confirm canary rollback window and customer-specific canary opt-in.

Diego Morales: Canary rollback default will be 15m with exponential backoff. We can add per-customer override, but need to surface it in rollout config and tests. ETA: prototype by Wed.

Nora Patel: SLA draft ready for legal review. Snippet here (WIP): "We guarantee 99.9% monthly availability for Dedicated clusters; latency targets and credits are detailed in the full SLA." Uploading full doc now. Link: https://redwood.internal/docs/sla/dedicated-ga :page_facing_up:

triage-bot: Incident summary posted: outage 4241078000 affected hosted-api-east-1 for ~28m. Root cause: autoscaler race condition under burst. Mitigation: rate limiter + throttle fix deployed 4241082000. Full postmortem: https://redwood.internal/incidents/4241078000 :clipboard:

Ava Chen: Thanks. Two notes from the postmortem: 1) add a synthetic test for autoscale bursts, 2) ensure oncall runbooks include the new throttle threshold. @Kai please own.

Kai Huang: On it. I'll add the synthetic test to the infra CI and bump the alert threshold to match the fix. Will post a PR by EOD Thu.

Maya Singh: Quick product note on EU retention: legal requested we default to 30d ephemeral KV and make longer retention opt-in during onboarding. This affects analytics pipelines and logs retention. We'll supply an opt-in flow for customers who need longer retention. Draft policy: https://redwood.internal/policies/eu-retention-v4 :lock:

Ava Chen: Good. Questions: 1) Impact on analytics exports? 2) Does this require changes to billing?

Diego Morales: Analytics exports will need explicit archival policy. Minimal billing change; we'll add a surcharge line for "extended retention" if customers opt in. Product + billing to coordinate.

Nora Patel: Legal asks that Dedicated customers handling regulated data be required to enable customer-managed KMS before GA. Add as onboarding gating checkbox.

hr-bot: Hiring update: SRE hiring freeze lifted. Requisition links posted in #people-ops. Priority roles: infra reliability, runtime performance.

Ava Chen: Small org asks: 1) hiring: hiring managers please confirm job postings by Mon COB, 2) docs: product/docs, please update Dedicated onboarding checklist with KMS and retention options by Tue.

Diego Morales: :thumbsup: will update rollout config and open PR for per-customer canary knobs.

Kai Huang: :thumbsup: adding synthetic test and alert changes.

Maya Singh: :thumbsup: will coordinate analytics archival plan with eng-ml.

Nora Patel: :thumbsup: legal will fast-track SLA review.

Ava Chen: Thanks team. I'll host a 15m exec sync Fri 3:30p if any triage is needed. If you cannot make it, drop blockers here. Closing note: p95 latency improved 12% month-over-month (nice work!) but we still have work on tail latencies. Keep focus on p99 for the next two sprints. :muscle:

Ava Chen: Quick micro-brief for the week — two short items: launch readiness for Private GA and a small but important tweak to our EU data handling. TL;DR: plan is on-track but we need a few quick confirmations by EOD Fri. :wave:

Ava Chen: Private GA: we’re green on infra capacity and rollout plan. Goals this week: finalize SLA language for Dedicated customers, lock EU retention defaults, and confirm automated rollback windows. Ask: owners pls ack below and call out any blockers.

Liam O'Neill: Ack. Working with infra to finalize the Dedicated SLA latency target. One ask: can we make the canary window configurable per-customer?

Ava Chen: @Liam — yes, that’s the ask from a few customers. Can we expose a knob in the rollout config? If it’s non-trivial, flag it now.

Marco Diaz: We can add a per-customer canary_duration field. Quick sketch in the rollout config:
```json
{"canary_duration": "PT30M", "fallback_model": "redwood-lite-v1"}
```
Will need 1 sprint to fully validate for Dedicated autoscale edge cases.

Priya Rao: On EU data handling: legal asked we default to 30d ephemeral KV cache for requests originating in EU by default and surface an opt-in for longer retention. Security + Compliance both prefer conservative default. I’ll follow up with a short RFC and config change.

Sasha Kwan: From product: need to update customer docs + pricing page if SLA targets change. ETA for doc updates: Tue next week if I get final SLA text by Fri.

legal-bot: New doc: /policies/eu-data-retention-draft-v3 uploaded by Priya Rao. Link: https://redwood.internal/docs/drafts/eu-retention-v3 :page_facing_up:

Priya Rao: @legal-bot — FYI that draft includes the opt-in flag and the admin UI text. Please review and call out redlines.

Ava Chen: Ops asks: 1) @Marco please confirm risk of canary_duration change on Dedicated autoscale (call out worst-case latency). 2) @Liam share SLA wording by Thu COB. 3) @Priya, @Sasha sync on customer-facing language — I want a single paragraph we can share with early access customers.

Marco Diaz: Will run a small load test today and post results in #eng-runtime by 6pm. If anything spikes >15% p50 we’ll revert plan for per-customer canary in this release.

Liam O'Neill: Draft SLA snippet (work-in-progress):
> We commit to 99.9% uptime for Dedicated clusters with best-effort latency targets of p95 < 200ms for standard models. See full terms in the SLA doc.
Will drop full text in /docs/sla/dedicated by Thu.

Sasha Kwan: I’ll prepare an FAQ entry and one short email template for EAP customers once I get the paragraph.

deploy-bot: Canary deployment for `private-ga/dedicated-rollout` started at 4241122400 — 3 nodes, region eu-west-1. Monitoring enabled.

Priya Rao: One more legal note: for Dedicated, we must ensure KMS keys are customer-managed at GA for regulated customers. That’s non-negotiable. Please include as checkbox in onboarding flow.

Ava Chen: Good catch. Add KMS checkbox to onboarding checklist and confirm with infra by Friday.

Marco Diaz: Noted. Will add a pre-flight check: `kms_configured == true` or else fail rollout.

Liam O'Neill: Quick ask from me: if we tighten EU retention default to 30d, what is the impact on analytics pipelines? @eng-ml / infra — please surface any downstream risks.

Priya Rao: Low-level analytics will need opt-in to persist beyond 30d; we’ll add an archival path. I’ll coordinate with eng-ml on mapping events to the archival flow.

Ava Chen: Thanks team. Final ask — everyone above, please react with :thumbsup: if you can meet the EOD Fri confirmations. I’ll do an exec quick call Fri 4p if we need to triage.

Liam O'Neill: :thumbsup:

Marco Diaz: :thumbsup:

Priya Rao: :thumbsup:

Sasha Kwan: :thumbsup:

Ava Chen: Awesome — will follow up with a short summary and next steps. If anyone hits a blocker, drop it here or ping me directly. :sparkles:

source.text:
Ava Chen: Quick weekly velocity note — short and actionable. Thanks team for the heavy lift on Dedicated capacity last week. A few high-level signals and asks for the next 7 days:

- Cost / performance: target a 5% net reduction in per-token spend across hosted tiers. Primary levers: batching tweaks, KV prefix caching, and enabling the new FP8 quantization profile where evals pass. We don't need perfect — move the needle.
- Private GA: security review is green pending two remaining KMS items. Legal needs the updated DPA by Fri EOD. Aim to open GA for select customers on Monday next week.
- Launch posture: canary rollout next Wed 10:00 PDT for the new routing policy (latency-first fallback). If canary looks clean, broad rollout Thu morning with staggered regional waves.
- Reliability: last Tuesday’s KV cache paste/reload caused increased tail latency. Jun and infra mitigations are in place; postmortem is scheduled for Fri.
- Freeze / coordination: freeze non-critical infra changes from Tue -> Fri of next week around canary window. If you need an exception, ping me + @Ravi.

Please keep updates short in this thread — one-line ack or exceptions. Thanks — big week. :rocket:

Ravi Patel: Ack — Eng will hold the infra freeze window and coordinate the canary playbook. I’ll post the canary checklist (brief) by EOD today.

Maya Lopez: Noted. PMs will hold marketing comms until we confirm GA date. Can Product Ops get the DPA draft link?

Ava Chen: @Maya I asked legal to drop the DPA in #legal-requests. Should arrive by 3pm.

Jun Park: Quick on the incident — root cause looks like an evicted KV-shard during an autoscale step; cascade on cache warmup. Mitigations: enforced shard pinning + a soft-warmup on scale-up. Postmortem Friday 11:00 in #incidents.

Kelsey (engops-bot): Action items created: 1) canary-checklist (Ravi) due today 18:00, 2) DPA upload (Legal) due today 15:00, 3) infra-freeze exceptions (Ava + Ravi) tracked. See /tasks/3639991111 for details. :white_check_mark:

Ravi Patel: Canary checklist draft posted:```
- verify canary capacity (GPU counts)
- smoke gen load test (5m)
- p50/p95 latency gating (p50 < 150ms, p95 < 450ms)
- error rate gating (<0.5%)
- rollback hooks verified
```

Maya Lopez: Thanks Ravi — Product will validate the smoke prompts and two customer-facing docs.

Ava Chen: Two tiny asks from me: 1) prioritize any quick batching/caching changes that are low risk (Ravi/Jun), 2) if GA slips, tell me asap with ETA and blocker.

Jun Park: Will prioritize. If we see unexpected tail on canary, we’ll pause and loop you in immediately.

Ravi Patel: One more — ops will surface cost projections after enabling FP8 on sampled models. Expect numbers by Mon morning.

Maya Lopez: Acknowledged. Will hold comms. :eyes:

Ava Chen: Awesome. Short updates only in this thread for the next 72h. Thank you everyone — we need tight coordination but this is doable. :muscle:

source.thread:
Ethan Park: Quick announcement — audiostream-v1 is going GA next Monday. This adds first-class audio streaming + tool-calling support (speech in, transcript + structured tool frames out). Endpoint: /v2/audio/stream, param model=audiostream-v1. Will enable new real-time voice assistants and call transcription flows. :loud_sound:

Sofia Martinez: Ops plan: canary tonight at 23:00 UTC (5% -> 30% -> 100% over 5h if stable). Key metrics: audio-p50-token, audio-p95-token, audio-decode-errors. SLOs: p50 < 160ms token, p95 < 1200ms. Auto-fallback goes to text-only worker `asr-compat-v1` on persistent decode failures.

deploy-bot: Canary started for audiostream-v1 — rollout ID roll-audio-22366. Stage: 5% (ETA 40m). Logs: https://internal.deploys/roll-audio-22366

Nia - PM: Customer comms: we'll send partners a preview today and public blog after 24h stable. Action items: docs + migration checklist ready by EOD Fri. @Sofia please confirm known limitations to include.

DocsBot: Docs available: https://docs.redwood.ai/api/audiostream-v1 — includes SDK examples and migration guide.

Sofia Martinez: Migration snippet (JS):
```
// old: client.openAudioStream('/v1/audio_stream')
client.generateStream({ model: 'audiostream-v1', inputs: [{type: 'audio', url: 'https://.../clip.wav'}], stream: true })
```
Old endpoint /v1/audio_stream will return header x-redwood-deprecate: v1-audio;expires=45d.

Rajiv - SRE: Runbooks updated: runbooks/audio/ -> asr-fallback, audio-queue-backpressure. Paging: eng-oncall when error-rate > 1.2% for 10m. Also added a short playbook for mismatched audio/frame ordering.

Ethan Park: Dedicated & Private note: Dedicated customers can opt to pin older artifacts; Private control planes get the endpoint on the next agent push — we won't auto-upgrade pinned instances.

deploy-bot: Canary progressed: 5% -> 30% (no critical alerts). Proceeding to 100% in 3h if metrics stay healthy. :rocket:

Sofia Martinez: Important perf callout: for low-latency voice use cases enable `compact-mode=true` to reduce memory pressure; this trades off minor quality for latency/cost. We'll default to compact-mode=false for GA but expose the flag.

Nia - PM: Webinar & Q&A scheduled Wed; invite will go out after 24h stable. I'll draft slides and ask engineering to join the demo.

DocsBot: Changelog entry created: https://docs.redwood.ai/changelog#audiostream-v1-ga. Known limitations section mentions 10s max chunking by default and 10MB per-audio soft limit.

Rajiv - SRE: Dashboards updated: Prod > audiostream-v1 > Overview. Shared Grafana once the rollout hits 100%.

Ethan Park: Thanks team. If you see any odd tool-call boundaries or audio trimming, paste logs here and tag `audio-stream-investigate`. :eyes:

source.messages:
Omar Aziz: FYI team — publishing the Private GDPR & Data Handling playbook today. This is for customers using Private/VPC installs who asked for clear guidance on subject requests, export controls, and telemetry settings.
Priya Rao: Runbook link: https://intra.redwood/runbooks/private-gdpr-playbook (draft). Contains step-by-step: responding to DSARs, configuring residency, and audit export samples.
Ben Carter: Key tech notes: we added two new controller endpoints: POST /dpa/acknowledge (for customer DPA acceptance) and GET /audit/export-status. Installer flag to disable telemetry by default is --telemetry=off. Example: 
```
./installer --mode private --telemetry=off --residency eu-central-1
```
Sofia N.: Legal + CSM: playbook includes email templates for DSAR acknowledgements and a customer checklist to prove data residency during audits. Please review the templates and suggest edits.
Omar Aziz: Ops: upgrades are online. For customers on agent <=v1.9 there is a small migration command: 
```
sudo redwood-agent backup-config && sudo redwood-agent upgrade --preserve-kv
```
Expect ~20-30m per node on average.
Priya Rao: Compliance note: default telemetry retention for new private installs is 30 days; customers can change to 0/30/90/365 via the Audit API. We added example payloads in the runbook.
Ben Carter: Security: Audit exports now include a signed provenance header. For on-prem HSMs, rotation automation remains opt-in and will emit an audit event with key metadata.
deploy-bot: Release schedule updated: playbook published -> 1956003600, customer emails to go out after legal sign-off at 1956089200. See #release-war-room for rollout.
Omar Aziz: Action items: 1) Legal & CSM (Sofia + Legal) finalise DSAR email text by EOD Wednesday. 2) Eng (Ben) verify installer flag in air-gapped bundle. 3) Ops (Priya) confirm per-node upgrade times from two pilot customers. Ping this thread when done.

source.messages:
Ava Chen: Morning all — quick executive snapshot for the week. High level: product momentum is strong, we closed two Dedicated deals, and we’re green on the Q2 roadmap. A few priorities and a short ops note below.

Ava Chen: Metrics highlights: MRR +8% WoW, new ARR booked ~$2.4M this week (two mid-enterprise deals), p95 inference latency 140ms, p99 430ms, platform uptime 99.95% last 7 days after mitigation. Cost/unit improvements: Optimize suggestions reduced token cost ~6% on average in pilot.

Ava Chen: Priorities for next week: 1) Finish Dedicated onboarding checklist for Acme & Nova (customer success + infra) 2) Ship RBAC + audit logs in Private GA 3) Push optimized quantization profiles to prod for high-throughput models 4) Roll out updated data retention default (see below).

Ava Chen: Ops note / incident summary — last Tue we had a partial outage impacting some Dedicated tenants during a scale event. Root cause: autoscaler race condition that double-scheduled workers for a small subset of pods, leading to temporary resource pressure and degraded routing. Incident resolved by rolling back the autoscale change and patching the scaler. Postmortem will be out EOD Friday.

Marco Ruiz: Thanks Ava. Quick ask — can we attach the slide deck with the numbers + churn breakout? I’ll share in leadership sync. :white_check_mark:

Priya Nair: From eng side — autoscaler patch landed (PR #4021) and deployed to canary cluster. Patch prevents duplicate scale actions and adds a short lock window. Added a metric to track duplicate-scaling attempts. Need infra to validate on dedicated pools.

Ben Holt: I validated on two dedicated pools. No duplicates observed in the canary run. Scheduling a full smoke this afternoon. We should also add an alert for rapid worker churn > 3 in 1m.

Sofia Park: Heads-up to DevEx & docs teams: we need a short KB and customer-facing blurb about the outage + what we changed. Will draft and post to status.redwood this morning.

Liam O'Connor: Financial impact estimate on the outage: preliminary hit ~ $38k in billable downtime for affected tenants, plus estimated $12k in support hours. Will update after final list of affected customers from CS.

Daniel Kim: On the RBAC + audit GA — what's the rollout cadence? Can we soft-launch to beta customers next week?

Ava Chen: Yes — beta for RBAC + audit logs next Wed (3 days). CS + Solutions will enable for 5 pilot accounts, then 2-week feedback loop before broader GA. Daniel, please own the pilot runbook.

Priya Nair: Action items: 1) Daniel - pilot runbook for RBAC (due Tue) 2) Ben - full smoke test on dedicated pools (today) 3) Sofia - status page copy + customer comms (this AM) 4) Priya - monitor canary and postmortem draft (EOD Fri).

Marco Ruiz: Small policy change to note: default data retention window will change to 30 days for new accounts, with opt-in for longer retention (90/365) under contract. This reduces surface for regulated customers by default and aligns with compliance ask. We’ll include a clear opt-in flow in Console.

Sofia Park: I’ll add the console text and an inline tooltip. Draft for review in #product this afternoon.

Daniel Kim: Noted on ownership. Will have runbook draft by EOD Tue.

Ben Holt: Smoke test passed on canary, no regressions observed. Scheduling broad rollout tonight at 22:00 PT; will monitor autoscaler metrics and rollback if we see duplicate-scaling > 0.

deploy-bot: deploy-bot: Canary deployment: redwood/autoscaler-service@patch-4021 completed — 1813456892 — status: success

Ava Chen: Thanks team — solid sprint. I’ll send a short exec note to folks not in this channel summarizing the GA windows, the policy change, and the postmortem link once it’s ready. Back to deep work. :sparkles:

source.body:
Dev Patel: Heads up — the refreshed Support/CS escalation playbook is now published in Confluence. Please bookmark + start using this for all customer-impacting escalations.

Primary hub (start here): https://confluence.redwoodinference.com/display/CSS/Escalation+Playbook

What’s new (highlights):
• Escalation paths + contacts (Hosted/Dedicated/Private + when to pull SRE vs Platform): https://confluence.redwoodinference.com/display/CSS/Escalation+Paths+and+Contacts+2026
• Severity matrix + escalation triggers (Support P0–P4 ↔ Incident Sev0–Sev3): https://confluence.redwoodinference.com/display/CSS/Severity+Matrix+and+Escalation+Triggers
• SLA policy by customer tier (first response + update cadence + after-hours rules): https://confluence.redwoodinference.com/display/CSS/SLA+Policy+by+Customer+Tier
• Slack bridge + incident channel conventions (naming + required context + when to move to #incidents): https://confluence.redwoodinference.com/display/CSS/Slack+Bridge+and+Incident+Channel+Conventions
• Executive escalation protocol + summary template: https://confluence.redwoodinference.com/display/CSS/Executive+Escalation+Protocol
• Customer comms templates (ack, mitigation updates, workaround guidance, resolution follow-up): https://confluence.redwoodinference.com/display/CSS/Customer+Comms+Templates

Expected adoption date: Monday 2026-02-17 (use for new SUP tickets/bridges created on/after that date; ok to keep older threads as-is).

Two asks:
1) Use the triage/intake checklist before paging (request IDs, region, model, SDK version, recent deploys): https://confluence.redwoodinference.com/display/CSS/Support+Triage+Intake+Checklist
2) When you open a bridge, paste the bridge template + link the SUP ticket (we’re rolling out automation to help).

Open questions / issues → reply in this thread or DM me. We’ll run 20 min office hours Tue/Thu this week to walk through changes.

(reactions: :tada: x18, :bookmark_tabs: x9, :eyes: x6)

Jasmine Liu: +1 — thanks Dev + everyone who reviewed. Please treat the update cadence language as “default policy” and loop Rachel early for any contract exceptions. :thumbsup:

Rachel Kim: Adding: for enterprise comms, please stick to the templates verbatim unless we’ve agreed otherwise in the bridge. It keeps us consistent across email + status page.

Sean Gallagher: SRE note: the severity matrix is aligned w/ paging thresholds we actually use today. If you’re debating P0 vs P1, err on the side of “page w/ complete context” vs “page early with missing details”. The required fields in the bridge template are there for a reason.

Rafael Mendes: Also if you move to #incidents, please make sure the bridge has at least 1 SUP linked + the customer impact summary (who/what/where). We lose too much time reconstructing context after the fact.

Dylan Brooks: Jira automations are in progress (INT-333). Starting next week you should see reminders for missing region/model/request IDs + an auto-prompt to link the known-issues index when relevant.

Elliot Price: Docs team will keep the “known issues” hub updated and we’ll continue to prune anything customer-specific. If you notice an article missing “when to escalate” guidance, tag @Docs in #docs and we’ll fix same day.

Owen Phillips: For Support: I’ll pin the bridge template in #support and update the internal macro library to point at these pages. If anyone hits a gap (e.g., routing/fallback confusion), send me the SUP and I’ll add it to the next KB batch.

Priya Shah: Quick q — are we standardizing the bridge channel name format now? (We’ve got a mix of `#bridge-*` and `#support-bridge-*` in the last month.)

Dev Patel: Yep — standard going forward:
• Customer escalation bridge started by Support: `#support-bridge-<customer>-<short-issue>-<mmdd>`
• If/when promoted to incident: incident bot will create the official `#inc-YYYYMMDD-<slug>` and we link back.
It’s in the Slack bridge conventions page above. :slightly_smiling_face:

Benji Okafor: Slack workflow update (INT-332) will enforce that naming format + auto-post the template + ask for SUP link + request IDs.

Sean Gallagher: Love it. Consistency helps during rotations.

(reactions: :white_check_mark: x12, :raised_hands: x7)

source.messages:
Marissa Cole: :tada: Auto-rollback on SLO breach (MVP) is now available for INTERNAL opt-in (behind a feature flag).

What it does (MVP):
• During canary/A-B/staged rollouts, we evaluate latency + error-rate SLO burn-rate for the rollout scope (route + model version + region + tier).
• If we detect a breach per policy, controller will auto-*pause* the rollout (default) or auto-*rollback* (if enabled in policy).
• Every decision is recorded in the rollout audit trail + appears in Console.

What it does NOT do (yet):
• Multi-region failover orchestration
• Quality/eval-based rollback signals

Docs / links:
• PRD (internal): https://confluence.redwoodinference.com/display/PROD/Auto-rollback+on+SLO+breach+integration
• Runbook (oncall + service owners): https://confluence.redwoodinference.com/display/ENGSRE/Auto-rollback+on+SLO+breach+runbook
• Metrics naming + labels: https://confluence.redwoodinference.com/display/ARCH/Rollouts+SLO+metrics+naming+and+labeling
• Default thresholds + templates (PM draft): https://drive.google.com/drive/folders/redwood/console-rollouts/auto-rollback-policy-options
• Dashboards/alerts PR (Grafana pack): https://github.com/observability-pack/pull/913

How to opt in (staging first; prod after SRE sign-off):
1) Confirm you have SLO monitors in slo-toolkit for:
   • p95 latency (fast+slow burn)
   • error rate (5xx)
   Monitors must be scoped to your rollout dimensions (route/model version/region). If you’re missing coverage, reply here and we’ll route to owners.
2) Enable the feature flag for your service:
   • `rollouts.auto_rollback.enabled=true` (service-level)
   • (Optional) `rollouts.auto_rollback.allow_rollback=true` (if you want rollback vs pause)
3) Add policy section to your rollout config (example below) OR use a template in Console Policy Editor (new section: “Auto-rollback (SLO)”).

Policy snippet (example):
```json
{
  "autoRollback": {
    "enabled": true,
    "action": "PAUSE",
    "signals": ["LATENCY_P95", "ERROR_RATE"],
    "windows": {"fast": "5m", "slow": "30m"},
    "debounce": "10m",
    "minRequests": 2000,
    "suppressDuringIncident": true
  }
}
```

Guardrails worth calling out:
• Minimum volume gate (avoids false positives on low traffic)
• Dual-window confirmation (fast+slow burn)
• Debounce/holdoff to prevent oscillation
• If monitors are stale/missing, controller will NOT rollback; it will log `monitor_missing_or_stale` and keep rollout in current state (and we’ll alert).

If you want to opt in, reply with:
• service + route
• model(s) + region(s)
• tier (hosted/dedicated/private)
• desired action (PAUSE vs ROLLBACK)
• owner + oncall channel

Owners for questions:
• Product/policy: @Paula Mendes
• Rollout controller: @Jada Williams / @Chloe Martin
• SLO semantics + oncall readiness: @Sean Gallagher / @Rafael Mendes

Thank you—this is a big reliability milestone.

Paula Mendes: Adding: Console UI is behind the same flag. If you don’t see the “Auto-rollback (SLO)” section in Policy Editor, ping me + include your org/service.

Jada Williams: Small clarification: rollback action currently uses a step-down strategy for traffic split (rate-limited) + is idempotent, but we’re still staging it per-service. If you’re the first prod adopter, expect tighter coordination.

Sean Gallagher: SRE note: for prod enablement we require (a) runbook link in your service doc, (b) dashboard access for oncall, (c) monitors verified non-stale for 7 days. We’ll do a quick 15m sign-off.

Aisha Bello: Dashboards are landing today; alerting is notify-only by default (no pages) until we tune thresholds. If your service opts in, we’ll add routing to the right Slack channel.

Connor O'Brien: Release note: feature flag defaults OFF in prod. Staging enablement is self-serve; prod enablement goes through ENG-4850 checklist. If you need help flipping flags, open a request in #eng-releases.

deploy-bot: Observability pack updated in staging: `observability-pack@v2.31.0` (includes rollout auto-rollback dashboards)

Felix Schneider: Audit trail: Console shows an “Auto action” event with decision outcome + suppression reason + links to monitor defs. If you’re missing links/fields, file a bug + include rollout_id.

Rafael Mendes: We’re running a game day next week (latency spike, 5xx burst, stale monitor). If your team wants to participate as a pilot adopter, reply and we’ll slot you in.

Marissa Cole: Thanks all. Pilot target: 2–3 internal services in staging this week, then 1 internal prod route next week. We’ll post follow-ups here with known issues / tuning changes.

Ethan Park: FYI: policy validation will reject unsafe configs (e.g., debounce < 5m, minRequests < 500). Error messages should point to the exact field.

Allison Grant: Please do NOT add free-form labels to the new metrics. Stick to the standard label set in the doc (cardinality limits). If you need an extra dimension, ask in #eng-sre first.

incident-bot: Reminder: auto-rollback events will post to Slack once incident-bot PR-477 is deployed to prod. Current staging notifications go to #eng-sre-test.

Paula Mendes: FAQ I’m getting in DMs: yes, you can set per-rollout overrides (e.g., stricter for a risky model version) and keep service defaults otherwise. UI will show both + which one won.

Chloe Martin: One more note: if controller can’t fetch SLO status (toolkit timeout), it will treat as “no signal” and log it. We’re tracking rates of this; please report if you see it frequently.

Marissa Cole: If you’re replying to opt in, please include your preferred window for enablement (this week vs next) so we can manage support load.

• During canary/A-B/staged rollouts, we evaluate latency + error-rate SLO burn-rate for the rollout scope (route + model version + region + tier).
• If we detect a breach per policy, controller will auto-*pause* the rollout (default) or auto-*rollback* (if enabled in policy).
• Every decision is recorded in the rollout audit trail + appears in Console.

What it does NOT do (yet):
• Multi-region failover orchestration
• Quality/eval-based rollback signals

Docs / links:
• PRD (internal): https://confluence.redwoodinference.com/display/PROD/Auto-rollback+on+SLO+breach+integration
• Runbook (oncall + service owners): https://confluence.redwoodinference.com/display/ENGSRE/Auto-rollback+on+SLO+breach+runbook
• Metrics naming + labels: https://confluence.redwoodinference.com/display/ARCH/Rollouts+SLO+metrics+naming+and+labeling
• Default thresholds + templates (PM draft): https://drive.google.com/drive/folders/redwood/console-rollouts/auto-rollback-policy-options
• Dashboards/alerts PR (Grafana pack): https://github.com/observability-pack/pull/913

How to opt in (staging first; prod after SRE sign-off):
1) Confirm you have SLO monitors in slo-toolkit for:
   • p95 latency (fast+slow burn)
   • error rate (5xx)
   Monitors must be scoped to your rollout dimensions (route/model version/region). If you’re missing coverage, reply here and we’ll route to owners.
2) Enable the feature flag for your service:
   • `rollouts.auto_rollback.enabled=true` (service-level)
   • (Optional) `rollouts.auto_rollback.allow_rollback=true` (if you want rollback vs pause)
3) Add policy section to your rollout config (example below) OR use a template in Console Policy Editor (new section: “Auto-rollback (SLO)”).

Policy snippet (example):
```json
{
  "autoRollback": {
    "enabled": true,
    "action": "PAUSE",
    "signals": ["LATENCY_P95", "ERROR_RATE"],
    "windows": {"fast": "5m", "slow": "30m"},
    "debounce": "10m",
    "minRequests": 2000,
    "suppressDuringIncident": true
  }
}
```

Guardrails worth calling out:
• Minimum volume gate (avoids false positives on low traffic)
• Dual-window confirmation (fast+slow burn)
• Debounce/holdoff to prevent oscillation
• If monitors are stale/missing, controller will NOT rollback; it will log `monitor_missing_or_stale` and keep rollout in current state (and we’ll alert).

If you want to opt in, reply with:
• service + route
• model(s) + region(s)
• tier (hosted/dedicated/private)
• desired action (PAUSE vs ROLLBACK)
• owner + oncall channel

Owners for questions:
• Product/policy: @Paula Mendes
• Rollout controller: @Jada Williams / @Chloe Martin
• SLO semantics + oncall readiness: @Sean Gallagher / @Rafael Mendes

Thank you—this is a big reliability milestone.

Paula Mendes: Adding: Console UI is behind the same flag. If you don’t see the “Auto-rollback (SLO)” section in Policy Editor, ping me + include your org/service.

Jada Williams: Small clarification: rollback action currently uses a step-down strategy for traffic split (rate-limited) + is idempotent, but we’re still staging it per-service. If you’re the first prod adopter, expect tighter coordination.

Sean Gallagher: SRE note: for prod enablement we require (a) runbook link in your service doc, (b) dashboard access for oncall, (c) monitors verified non-stale for 7 days. We’ll do a quick 15m sign-off.

Aisha Bello: Dashboards are landing today; alerting is notify-only by default (no pages) until we tune thresholds. If your service opts in, we’ll add routing to the right Slack channel.

Connor O'Brien: Release note: feature flag defaults OFF in prod. Staging enablement is self-serve; prod enablement goes through ENG-4850 checklist. If you need help flipping flags, open a request in #eng-releases.

deploy-bot: Observability pack updated in staging: `observability-pack@v2.31.0` (includes rollout auto-rollback dashboards)

Felix Schneider: Audit trail: Console shows an “Auto action” event with decision outcome + suppression reason + links to monitor defs. If you’re missing links/fields, file a bug + include rollout_id.

Rafael Mendes: We’re running a game day next week (latency spike, 5xx burst, stale monitor). If your team wants to participate as a pilot adopter, reply and we’ll slot you in.

Marissa Cole: Thanks all. Pilot target: 2–3 internal services in staging this week, then 1 internal prod route next week. We’ll post follow-ups here with known issues / tuning changes.

Ethan Park: FYI: policy validation will reject unsafe configs (e.g., debounce < 5m, minRequests < 500). Error messages should point to the exact field.

Allison Grant: Please do NOT add free-form labels to the new metrics. Stick to the standard label set in the doc (cardinality limits). If you need an extra dimension, ask in #eng-sre first.

incident-bot: Reminder: auto-rollback events will post to Slack once incident-bot PR-477 is deployed to prod. Current staging notifications go to #eng-sre-test.

Paula Mendes: FAQ I’m getting in DMs: yes, you can set per-rollout overrides (e.g., stricter for a risky model version) and keep service defaults otherwise. UI will show both + which one won.

Chloe Martin: One more note: if controller can’t fetch SLO status (toolkit timeout), it will treat as “no signal” and log it. We’re tracking rates of this; please report if you see it frequently.

Marissa Cole: If you’re replying to opt in, please include your preferred window for enablement (this week vs next) so we can manage support load.

Chris Osei: :mega: Marketplace Listing Refresh is LIVE (AWS + GCP)

Big thanks to everyone across PMM/Partnerships/Eng/Security/Docs/RevOps. Starting today we’re moving off the legacy marketplace listings and onto the refreshed offers + onboarding flow.

What changed (buyer-facing):
1) New marketplace plan/SKU naming (consistent across AWS + GCP)
   • Hosted API (Usage-based)
   • Dedicated Capacity (Reserved)
   • Private Deployment (VPC)
   Add-ons:
   • Compliance Pack
   • Premium Support

2) Listing content refreshed
   • Clear Hosted vs Dedicated vs Private differentiation
   • Updated screenshots + "How it works" section
   • Procurement-friendly FAQ (billing, entitlement timing, support)

3) Marketplace onboarding hardening (subscription → entitlement → org provisioning → first API key)
   • New console marketplace entrypoint
   • Entitlement verification API + improved error states
   • Entitlement sync job retries + alerting

4) Security documentation bundle for marketplace review
   • Marketplace-friendly security overview + shared responsibility
   • Data handling/retention + residency notes
   • Audit logging overview + export options

Where to find enablement (internal):
- PMM copy / positioning blocks (approved language): confluence → Sales Enablement → Positioning: "Marketplace positioning + copy guidelines"
- Procurement one-pager (when to route to marketplace vs direct): confluence → Sales Enablement → Pitch Decks: "Marketplace procurement one-pager"
- Customer-facing FAQ draft (billing, entitlements, trials/credits): drive → GTM → "customer-facing-marketplace-faq-draft"
- Security packet index (what’s public vs gated): confluence → Security & Compliance → "Marketplace security documentation pack"
- Deal ops + required HubSpot fields: drive → RevOps → "revops-process-marketplace-deal-ops"

Escalation / help:
- Provisioning/entitlement issues (stuck linking org, missing entitlement): #support (tag @Dev Patel) + open SUP ticket
- Billing dimension mismatch / SKU mapping questions: #eng-platform (tag @Cole Summers)
- Security questionnaire / evidence requests: #eng-security (tag @Naomi Feldman) + loop in @Olga Petrov if evidence gating needed

If you’re customer-facing this week: please route marketplace buyers to the new onboarding docs + FAQ first. We want to reduce “time-to-first-token” + support load.

Dropping a quick checklist in thread next.

Camila Reyes: +1 and please use the new screenshots/copy only (don’t pull from the old listing pages). If you see outdated assets floating around, ping me or @Soojin Lee.

Soojin Lee: Quick note on language: avoid saying “SOC2 compliant” in emails/decks. Use the approved phrasing from the copy guidelines (e.g., “SOC 2 Type II report available under NDA”) + link the security packet index.

Jordan Blake: This is a big unlock for procurement-led deals. Thank you all. AEs: if you have an account that *must* buy via marketplace, please re-check the right plan name before you send them the subscribe link.

Alex Martinez: Docs are live + nav updated. Marketplace onboarding landing pages are now under Docs → “Marketplace”. If you hit any broken redirects or weird 404s, file INT ticket + tag me.

Elliot Price: Also: security packet publication is split (public vs gated). If a buyer asks for evidence (SOC2, pen test attestation, etc.), don’t forward random PDFs. Use the “Security packet index” page and request access via Security/GRC.

Chloe Martin: Onboarding UX notes:
- If buyer already has a Redwood org, the console now prompts to link subscription → existing org (instead of creating a duplicate)
- If entitlement hasn’t propagated yet, they’ll see a “Try again” state with guidance (and the backend retries are more aggressive now)
- If it’s been >30 min, please escalate (we have alerts but want human eyes fast)

Cole Summers: Billing/metering heads-up:
- Hosted dimensions are now buyer-friendly labels (Text generation input/output, Embeddings, Rerank)
- Dedicated includes Reserved GPU hours (+ Usage overage where applicable)
- If you see a line item that looks wrong, grab the marketplace subscription ID + Redwood org ID and tag me in #eng-platform.

Nadia Rahman: Telemetry is in place for the onboarding funnel. We’re tracking:
- entitlement lookup latency
- org linking failures
- API key creation completion
- first successful request
If you want dashboard access, ping me/Ben.

Ben Carter: Baseline conversion metrics are in the GTM drive folder ("marketplace-onboarding-funnel-baseline-metrics"). We’ll post a weekly update after 1 full week of data.

Naomi Feldman: Security escalation guardrails:
- Standard questionnaires: start with the draft response doc + cite the security packet pages
- Anything requesting raw evidence artifacts (reports, detailed diagrams, vuln scan output): loop in Security before sending
- Residency/retention questions: use the marketplace data handling + retention page (it has the current defaults + configurable options)

Dev Patel: Support macros are ready for:
- “Subscription active but no entitlement”
- “Provisioning delayed >30 minutes”
- “Billing dimension question”
We’ll pin them in #support. If an enterprise buyer is blocked during procurement/onboarding, escalate quickly — we want a clean first week.

Stephanie Nguyen: Field note: buyers will ask “what did I buy?” during org linking. The new plan names are much clearer. Please copy/paste exactly:
Hosted API (Usage-based) / Dedicated Capacity (Reserved) / Private Deployment (VPC)

Lukas Meyer: EMEA teams: Private Deployment (VPC) language is landing well with security reviewers. If anyone gets pressure for “on-prem in marketplace” specifically, flag it — we should route those to Private direct contracting.

Chris Osei: Quick internal checklist (customer-facing):
1) Confirm which marketplace (AWS vs GCP) + which plan name
2) Send: onboarding doc link + FAQ + (if needed) security packet index
3) If provisioning is stuck: collect subscription ID + Redwood org ID + timestamp, open SUP ticket, tag @Dev Patel
4) If pricing/billing questions: route to one-pager + RevOps deal ops doc, tag @Isabelle Laurent as needed

Please reply here if you hit any rough edges today so we can capture + fix fast.

Ben Carter: :rocket: Rolling out Lifecycle Growth Experiments (week 2 launch)

Starting today we’re shipping:
1) Onboarding email drip v2 (new users) — A/B test (Variant A vs B)
2) Initial in-console onboarding nudge experiment (behind flags)

Why: reduce time-to-first-token + lift activation (% of new signups hitting first successful request + first 100k tokens in 7 days).

What’s live (high-level):
• Email v2: refreshed copy + CTAs aligned to the minimal “hello world” request + quickstart links
• Console: “Next step” nudges for: create API key → make first request → enable streaming → view usage dashboard

Guardrails / how we’re doing this safely:
• Deliverability ramp: starting at low volume and scaling daily if complaint/bounce stays within thresholds
• Suppression: no sends to unsubscribed users, existing active users, and anyone flagged as sales-assisted/active opp (per exclusions)
• Holdout: a % of eligible new signups will be held out for clean measurement
• Stop/go: we pause if we see deliverability issues or meaningful negative movement in activation funnel

Opt-out / compliance reminders:
• All lifecycle emails include unsubscribe link (CAN-SPAM) + we respect preferences immediately
• We now have a Console email preferences surface (Settings → Email preferences) so users can manage onboarding/lifecycle sends
• GDPR/data handling: audience membership is based only on approved product events + retention limits per policy

Where to monitor:
• Activation Funnel Dashboard: https://observability.redwood.local/d/activation-funnel (signup → api key → first request → 100k tokens)
• Email metrics (opens/clicks/complaints): https://console.redwood.local/marketing/email-health

Escalation paths:
• Deliverability/complaints: @Tyler Benson + @Ben Carter (and #marketing)
• Console nudge bugs/UX regressions: @Marissa Cole + @Dylan Brooks (and #product)
• Data/metrics discrepancies: @Nadia Rahman + @Sergio Costa (and #eng-platform)

If you notice anything weird (duplicate emails, missing emails, nudges showing up in the wrong place), reply in thread with screenshots + user/org id if you have it. Thanks all.

Camila Reyes: Thanks Ben. +1 on watching the guardrails closely this week. If we hit deliverability warning signs, we pause first and debug second.

Marissa Cole: Console nudge is live for the targeted cohort only (new signups, eligible routes). Placement is: Console home + API Keys page. Dismissal should persist per user.

Dylan Brooks: Flags for anyone curious:
• `lifecycle_growth_email_v2_ab` (cohort assign + holdout)
• `console_onboarding_nudges_v1` (nudge eligibility)
We’re at 10% ramp on console exposure right now. Will move to 25% tomorrow if error rate stays flat.

Tyler Benson: Deliverability note: we’re throttling at the MTA + monitoring complaint rate hourly. If you see anyone internal not receiving, check spam first but also drop the address in thread and I’ll verify suppression + DMARC alignment.

Olga Petrov: Reminder from compliance side: please don’t manually add anyone to an audience outside the approved rules (especially EU users). If a customer asks about why they’re seeing onboarding emails, point them to Email preferences + we can share the data handling summary.

Michael Grant: +1. Also ensure the “from” name + physical address footer stays intact in the templates. Don’t edit copy directly in the sending tool without a review loop.

Allison Grant: Dashboard panels include alerts for sudden activation drops post-launch. If we see a step-change in first request completion, we’ll page #eng-platform for validation.

Sergio Costa: FYI: there’s a known 10–15 min lag on the funnel dashboard right now. Not a regression, just pipeline delay. If you see missing events beyond that window, tag me.

Leila Farouk: QA checklist for console nudges is running now (cross-browser + mobile). If anyone hits a nudge blocking the API key flow, please treat as P0 and ping me + Dylan.

Nadia Rahman: Instrumentation sanity checks: signup + api_key_created are deduped; first_request is per org+model+day. If you’re comparing raw event counts vs funnel uniques, that’s why.

Support Bot: Heads up: Support will monitor for any spike in “didn’t receive onboarding email” tickets today/tomorrow and route to @Tyler Benson + @Ben Carter. If you see customer complaints in Slack, please link the ticket.

Ben Carter: Appreciate all. We’ll post a short status update in #announcements end of day with: ramp %, any incidents, and early directional metrics (no overfitting to tiny samples). Keep the reports coming.

source.messages:
Connor O'Brien: :mega: SDK parity update is shipping today: standardized **auth + retries/backoff + timeouts + streaming semantics** across Python / TypeScript / Go.

Why: we’ve had too many “works in TS but not in Python” issues around 429s, timeouts, and stream disconnect behavior. This release aligns defaults + configuration knobs, and makes errors / request_id surfaced consistently.

✅ Released versions
• Python: `redwood-sdk` **v1.9.0**
• TypeScript: `@redwood-ai/sdk` **v2.4.0**
• Go: `github.com/redwood-ai/redwood-go` **v1.6.0**

What changed (high level)
1) Auth resolution (now consistent)
• Precedence: explicit client param > env var(s) > (legacy env vars w/ deprecation warning)
• Env vars (preferred): `REDWOOD_API_KEY` (API key) or `REDWOOD_BEARER_TOKEN` (bearer)
• Legacy names still work for now but will warn in logs (no secret values printed).

2) Retry policy (now consistent)
• Retries enabled by default for **429 + 5xx + transient network errors**
• Honors `Retry-After` (seconds + HTTP-date formats)
• Exponential backoff w/ jitter, capped, and bounded max attempts (defaults tuned to avoid thundering herd)
• Typed errors now include `request_id`, `status_code`, and `retryable` where available.

3) Timeouts (now consistent)
• Unified model: connect vs read vs overall timeout (mapped per language client)
• Defaults adjusted to be production-safe; streaming has distinct behavior (see below)

4) Streaming semantics (now consistent)
• If a stream disconnects mid-flight, SDKs surface a typed streaming disconnect error and include `request_id` when present.
• SDKs do **not** automatically retry once any partial output has been received (to avoid duplicate side effects / billing confusion). Docs include safe retry guidance + idempotency notes.

Docs / migration notes
• Migration guide (config changes, deprecated env vars, examples): https://confluence.redwoodinference.com/display/ENGDX/sdk-migration-notes-auth-retry-timeout
• Standard (retry/timeout details): https://confluence.redwoodinference.com/display/ARCH/sdk-retry-timeout-standard
• Streaming quickstart (disconnect handling + timeouts): https://confluence.redwoodinference.com/display/ENGDX/streaming-quickstart-consistent-behavior

Rollout notes
• These are intended to be low-breakage changes. Where behavior may differ materially (esp. timeouts), the migration guide calls it out + shows how to restore old behavior.
• Please route any “this changed my production behavior” reports to Ibrahim + the language owner with SDK version + `request_id` if you have it.

Big thanks to Monica / Ibrahim / Sabrina / Dana / Mateusz + SRE + Security + Docs for getting this over the line. :tada:

Monica Patel: Thanks everyone. This is the first release gated by the cross-language conformance suite, so we should see fewer regressions going forward.

Ibrahim Khan: +1. If you hit anything weird, please include:
- SDK + version
- whether it was streaming vs non-streaming
- the `request_id` (now included on most raised errors)
- and if you were behind a proxy / corp network (re: stream disconnects)

Sabrina James: Python install/upgrade:
`pip install -U redwood-sdk`
Config options are in the migration doc, but tl;dr `timeout`, `max_retries`, and `retry_backoff` are now the same names/semantics as TS/Go.

Dana Lo: TS upgrade:
`npm i @redwood-ai/sdk@^2.4.0`
Note: we kept the older env var aliasing but you’ll see a one-time deprecation warning on startup if you’re using the old names.

Mateusz Zielinski: Go upgrade:
`go get github.com/redwood-ai/redwood-go@v1.6.0`
Context cancellation now reliably aborts both HTTP and streaming reads (we had some gaps there before).

Allison Grant: SRE note: during rollout please keep an eye on retry rate + 429s; defaults are designed to be conservative, but if you see spiky behavior in a service, ping #eng-sre. Dashboards are live with dimensions for `retry_count`, `timeout_ms`, and streaming disconnect reasons.

Marcus Lin: Security reminder: please do *not* log raw env vars or headers. The SDKs redact secrets by default; if you’ve wrapped clients with your own debug logging, make sure you’re not dumping `Authorization`.

Wei Chen: Docs updates are live as of this morning; if you find an example that still uses old env var names, drop a note in #docs and we’ll patch quickly.

Elliot Price: Also, if you maintain internal quickstarts/templates, please update them to the new env var names + consistent timeout knobs. We saw a few internal repos copying older snippets.

ReleaseBot: Published packages:
- redwood-sdk 1.9.0 (PyPI)
- @redwood-ai/sdk 2.4.0 (npm)
- redwood-go v1.6.0 (git tag)

Connor O'Brien: :thumbsup: We’ll post a follow-up in ~1 week with early impact (support tickets, retry/timeout metrics, and any hotfixes).

Talia Benson: Heads up: we’ve shipped new customer-facing incident comms templates + a short training rollout for everyone who rotates as IC/Comms Lead/Scribe.

What’s changing (starting next week):
• Standardized Statuspage update blocks for each phase: Investigating / Identified / Mitigating / Monitoring / Resolved (+ retro link).
• New expectation: component-accurate updates (product surface + region + capability) — no more “API” blanket when it’s just streaming/embeddings.
• Incident-bot now requires fields for component_id + region + capability during intake, so drafts can be accurate.

Docs:
• Incident update templates (Confluence): https://confluence.redwood.local/display/ONCALL/Incident+Communication+Templates
• Status page update runbook: https://confluence.redwood.local/display/ONCALL/Status+Page+Update+Runbook
• Roles/approval notes (Comms Lead, Sev0/Sev1 approval): https://confluence.redwood.local/display/ONCALL/Incident+Comms+Roles+and+Responsibilities

Training (30 min, recorded):
1) Tues 11:00–11:30 PT — “TTFU + using the templates in practice”
2) Thu 14:00–14:30 PT — “Tagging components/regions correctly + anti-flap + approval flow”
Calendar invites going out today. Office hours Fri 10:00–10:30 PT for Q&A.

Reminder on tagging:
• If impact is limited: pick the smallest accurate scope (e.g., “Hosted API – Streaming – us-east” rather than “Hosted API”).
• If you don’t know yet, that’s ok — still pick your best initial component/region, and update as we learn more.
• Avoid naming specific customers or capacity constraints in public updates.

If you hit a weird mapping edge case, reply here w/ the incident link and we’ll fix the registry.

Rafael Mendes: +1 to all of this. Goal is faster + more consistent updates, not extra burden. TTFU target stays: first public update within 15 min for Sev0/Sev1 when customer impact is likely.

Christine Wong: The templates have “safe” wording baked in for early uncertainty. Please don’t freestyle unless there’s a strong reason — it makes accuracy audits way harder.

Devon Price: incident-bot change is live now. You’ll see new prompts in #incidents intake. If you get blocked by missing permissions for Statuspage publish, file INT + tag me.

Dev Patel: Support side: this will help a lot. Biggest pain lately has been “status says all green” while embeddings/streaming is clearly failing. Please make sure the component/region is right before hitting publish.

Wei Chen: Minor tone note: we’re trying to keep updates factual + actionable (what’s impacted, mitigation steps, next update timing). Avoid speculation about root cause until we’re sure.

Allison Grant: For verification, dashboards are linked from the runbook now by component. If you’re unsure whether it’s truly region-scoped, check the per-region error/latency panels before marking global.

Aisha Bello: Also: if it’s latency-only degradation, we should still mark “Degraded Performance” if it crosses the threshold sustained window (anti-flap rules apply). Don’t wait for errors if customers are timing out.

Miles Thornton: For folks worried about extra steps during a fire: use the incident-bot draft -> paste into Statuspage -> done. The goal is fewer back-and-forth edits in-thread.

Naomi Feldman: Security/compliance reminder: no internal hostnames, no provider-specific incident IDs, no customer names. If you’re unsure, ask Comms Lead to route through the Sev0/Sev1 approval step.

Michael Grant: Legal note: “we will prevent this from happening again” is too strong. Use “we’re implementing follow-ups to reduce likelihood/impact” language.

incident-bot: :information_source: New incident intake fields required: component_id, region, capability.
incident-bot: :information_source: Comms Lead reminders enabled. First public update target: 15 minutes (Sev0/Sev1).

Elliot Price: Docs note: we’ll update the public-facing “How to read our status page” article once the new component layout is fully rolled out. Internal templates are the source of truth for now.

Talia Benson: Thanks all. If you can’t make either session, watch the recording (link will be added to the Confluence page). Please react with :thumbsup: once you’ve read the templates + runbook.

Rafael Mendes: :thumbsup:

Dev Patel: :thumbsup:

Allison Grant: :thumbsup:

Devon Price: :thumbsup: (also DM me if you see a component missing — we’ve already got one fix queued for embeddings vs chat classification)

Christine Wong: :thumbsup:

Ava Chen: Quick pulse as we kick off the quarter — a few high-level asks and context for the org. TL;DR: focus on reliability, private deployments, and cost per token.

Ravi Patel: thanks ava — can you call out the priority order? what shifts from last quarter?

Ava Chen: Priority 1: SLA and reliability (p95 latency & error budget). Priority 2: Private/VPC launches for regulated customers. Priority 3: Unit cost improvements (batching + quant suggestions). We will also pause non-critical infra experiments for 6 weeks to stabilize.

Maria Gomez: noting product asks: we need a clear canary checklist for Private so sales + CE can sign contracts. I'll share a draft by EOD Tue.

Jules Carter: on reliability — recent infra rollout reduced p95 by ~12% in hosted for seq len 512. Still seeing region-specific tail errors in eu-west-2. oncall-bot, any incidents this morning?

oncall-bot: 1759991142 Incident detected: transient gRPC timeout spike in eu-west-2 (00:07 - 00:21 UTC). Affected ~0.2% of requests. Root cause: kernel upgrade + networking driver mismatch on 3 hosts. Remediation: rollback of driver and rolling restart. Incident closed.

Ava Chen: good catch. Jules — please own postmortem and mitigation plan (driver upgrade gating). We need a short blameless exec summary by Fri.

Jules Carter: on it. will run RCA and share runbook updates.

Ravi Patel: cost note: Optimize found improvements on batching suggestions — estimated -8% token cost if we enable adaptive prefix caching for high-throughput routes. We'll start a canary on dedicated pools next week.

Maria Gomez: Sales update: APAC private beta has 3 signed pilot customers. We need approval to enable region-specific KMS integrations. Security signoff pending.

Ava Chen: approve proceeding with KMS integrations for the APAC pilots, conditional on Security checklist completion. @DevEx Bot — please add KMS checklist to the Private deployment template.

DevEx Bot: Added checklist item: "APAC KMS integration verified (keys rotated, policies audited)" to Private deployment template #templates.

Ava Chen: org asks: 1) No new major infra experiments for 6 weeks. 2) Every team has a single measurable OKR tied to reliability/cost by next Mon. 3) Exec will review canary checklists for Private launches.

Ravi Patel: will align ENG OKRs, will send proposals by Fri.

Maria Gomez: product will align launch docs and legal/SEC requirements.

Jules Carter: I'll publish the postmortem template and schedule a runbook walkthrough with oncall next Wed.

Ava Chen: thanks all. Small wins this week: hosted p95 down 12%, unit cost down 8% in canaries. Keep comms tight — escalate anything that risks SLAs. :rocket:

Camila Reyes: It’s live. :mega:

Redwood’s **Latency/$ Benchmark Report** is now public.

**Landing page + interactive tables:** https://redwoodinference.com/benchmarks/latency-per-dollar
**Download PDF:** https://redwoodinference.com/benchmarks/latency-per-dollar/report.pdf
**Methodology (public):** https://redwoodinference.com/benchmarks/latency-per-dollar/methodology

Huge shoutout to Applied ML, Runtime, Infra, Web, PMM, Legal, and Sales Enablement for getting this over the line. Please use this thread for quick questions + what you’re seeing in the wild.

Key ask: if you share externally, please stick to the approved talk track + don’t ad-lib comparative claims beyond what’s in the report.

Soojin Lee: Congrats everyone. :raised_hands:

**Internal enablement kit (Sales/SE/CS):**
- Core message house + disallowed phrasing: https://confluence.redwoodinference.com/display/SE/benchmark-report-core-message-house
- FAQ + objection handling: https://confluence.redwoodinference.com/display/SE/benchmark-report-faq
- Competitive notes (safe language): https://confluence.redwoodinference.com/display/SE/benchmark-competitive-notes
- Internal launch deck (for customer calls): https://confluence.redwoodinference.com/display/SE/benchmark-report-launch-deck
- One-pager (send to AEs/SDRs): https://drive.google.com/drive/folders/redwood-gtm/benchmark-one-pager

**CTA guidance (quick):**
- Developers / PLG: push landing page + “try Hosted API”
- Enterprise / security / SLOs: “Dedicated sizing” or “Private/VPC demo” (don’t over-index on a single chart)

If you’re unsure how to position for a specific prospect, tag @Markus Klein or @Stephanie Nguyen / @Lukas Meyer.

Ethan Wallace: Adding press/social assets for anyone who needs them:
- Approved visuals + social copy snippets: https://drive.google.com/drive/folders/redwood-gtm/benchmark-press-kit
- Blog post (public): https://redwoodinference.com/blog/latency-per-dollar-benchmarks

If you see comments about “fairness” or “cherry-picking,” please link methodology first and avoid debating in threads. We have a short response template in the FAQ.

Jonas Weber: From the technical side: please route methodology questions to the public methodology page above.

Two reminders that came up in reviews:
- We publish **p50/p95 + latency/$ + $/1M tokens**, not just a single “winner” number.
- The report is a **defined workload mix** (prefill/decode) with stated concurrency + warmups; results will vary by prompt distribution.

If a prospect wants to replicate, we have a lightweight “PoC replication notes” section in the enablement FAQ.

Markus Klein: For SEs doing live walkthroughs: use the interactive table first (filters + tooltips), then PDF for narrative.

Mini demo flow that’s been working:
1) Pick their region + model family
2) Toggle p95 latency + latency/$
3) Talk about Dedicated/Private options for isolation + predictable throughput

Also: if someone asks “is caching enabled?” don’t guess—use the exact language in methodology.

Stephanie Nguyen: For NA AEs/SDRs: the recommended first-touch email is in Outreach as **“Benchmark Launch - Latency/$”** (updated this morning). Please don’t forward the draft doc versions.

If you get replies asking “can you guarantee these numbers?”, the correct answer is: we guarantee **SLA targets** on Dedicated (latency/uptime) and we can run a PoC in their region/workload to validate.

Lukas Meyer: EMEA note: we’re getting immediate questions on data residency. Please stick to:
- “We offer EU regions; see the region selector in the interactive table.”
- “For stricter requirements, Redwood Private (VPC/on-prem) is available; we can align on residency + controls.”

Do not claim “all data stays in X” unless the deal has the right deployment + contract language.

Grace O'Connor: SDR routing: inbound demo requests from the benchmark page are tagged **Campaign = Benchmark Launch** in HubSpot. If you see one untagged, please drop the link in #revops-help.

Jasmine Liu: CS/support heads-up: expect tickets asking “why did my run differ from the report?”

Suggested response:
- Point to methodology + note workload variance
- Offer to help them run a small test with their prompt set
- Escalate anything that smells like a public claim discrepancy to #benchmark-war-room

Sean Gallagher: SRE note: we’re monitoring landing page + JSON results service closely this week.

If you see the public page loading slowly or errors, please post in #eng-sre with:
- URL
- approx time
- screenshot

(We’ve got dashboards + alerts live.)

LaunchBot: Launch week schedule (PT)
- Tue 10:00am: Community post + HN monitoring
- Wed 9:00am: Lifecycle email send (PLG)
- Thu 11:00am: Webinar (live demo + Q&A)
- Fri 2:00pm: Sales/SE office hours (internal)

Full calendar: https://drive.google.com/drive/folders/redwood-gtm/benchmark-editorial-calendar

Camila Reyes: Final note: please amplify, but be precise. If you’re posting externally, use the approved visuals and link the landing page (not random screenshots).

Proud of this team.

source.messages:
Ava Chen: Morning — quick exec micro-brief. Thanks for the steady ops last week. Two asks: 1) tighten cost targets for hosted tier by 8% y/y on per-token spend — ops+eng, prioritize caching and batching recommendations; 2) we need SLA recalibration for Dedicated customers in EU region after infra change. Details below.

Diego Morales: Ack. Eng will push a batching/caching quick playbook today. Can we relax p99 from 220ms -> 250ms temporarily while we apply kernel patches? Need signoff from product/ops.

Riley Gupta: Product supports a short p99 relaxation for the next 2 weeks if we: a) notify impacted customers, b) enable automatic fallbacks to lower-cost model variants. I'll own customer comms with CS.

Samir Patel: For kernel patches, can we schedule a rolling canary tonight 02:00-04:00 PST? deploy-bot will manage. Patch reduces memory pressure but needs a DB migration in read-only mode.

deploy-bot: Scheduled canary deploy: canary-2024-1741239999 -> infra: gpu-pool-3 | window 02:00-04:00 PST | rollback on error. Link: https://deploys.redwood/example/1741239999

Maya Li: Legal/Compliance: note on data-retention policy — any change to caching must still honor per-customer retention TTLs and KMS scoping. Ops, please confirm no cached data is stored outside requested TTLs.

Diego Morales: Confirmed — caching will be prefix-only and honor TTL. We'll add telemetry to show cache-hit demographics. Patch includes flag: cache.store_policy=prefix_ttl.

Kara O'Neil: Heads up — we're deferring the company-wide all-hands to Monday to give teams time to adjust. People Ops will send updated calendar.

Ava Chen: Thanks all. Final asks: Diego/Riley — get a customer-facing note by EOD PST; Samir — proceed with canary but pause migration if error >= 3% of requests; Maya — confirm compliance signoff by EOD. I'll review metrics Tues morning. :thumbsup: 

Riley Gupta: On it, drafting message now.

Maya Li: Compliance signoff issued, pending small audit — will post evidence in #compliance by EOD.

Samir Patel: Canary started. Monitoring dashboard: https://metrics.redwood/canary/1741239999 — will post updates here.

Connor O'Brien: :loudspeaker: Runtime LTS pilot is live (internal) — starting this week

We’re kicking off a pilot for a new *Runtime Long Term Support (LTS) channel* aimed at Dedicated + Private customers that need a stable serving runtime baseline (kernel + scheduling stability, predictable patch cadence, slower-change release notes).

**What this changes (for Eng + SRE + Support/CS):**
• We now have an LTS release branch: `runtime-1.18-lts` (separate from mainline)
• Patch cadence target: *monthly* patches + *out-of-band* security patches when needed
• Backports into LTS are tightly controlled (security + Sev1/Sev2 correctness + critical perf regressions only)
• LTS patches ship with enterprise-grade release notes (compat matrix / known issues / upgrade & rollback notes / perf deltas)

**Pilot scope:**
• LTS line: `1.18.x-lts` (first pilot patch targeted: `1.18.1-lts`)
• Deployment modes: Dedicated + Private first (Hosted will follow once we validate gates + comms)
• Perf gates are mandatory before publish (perf-canary + scheduler smoke suite + release-branch CI)

**Who’s involved / owners:**
• Release lead: Connor O’Brien (@connor)
• Program manager: Talia Benson (@talia)
• Exec sponsor: Mateo Alvarez (@mateo)
• SRE ownership/on-call: Sean Gallagher (@sean) + Rafael Mendes (@rafael)
• CI/build: Sahana Iyer (@sahana)
• Kernel stability: Hiro Tanaka (@hiro)
• Scheduling stability: Sofia Ivanova (@sofia)
• Observability/perf signals: Allison Grant (@allison)
• Release notes/docs: Elliot Price (@elliot)
• Customer pilot coordination: Rachel Kim (@rachel) + Support intake: Dev Patel (@dev)

**How to request LTS pilot access (internal):**
1) If you have a customer asking for LTS (or a deal blocker), please post in `#eng-releases` with:
   • customer name + deployment type (Dedicated/Private)
   • requested support window + cadence expectations
   • current runtime version + why they can’t follow mainline
   • any existing tickets / incident refs
2) AEs/CSMs: also loop in @rachel and your deal owner; we’ll confirm eligibility + timelines.

**How to request an LTS backport (Support/Eng):**
• File a backport request via Support intake (or link the existing ticket) and include:
  – severity (Sev1/Sev2/security)
  – repro steps + logs (or minimal repro)
  – affected versions + whether workaround exists
  – perf impact if applicable
• We’ll triage in weekly LTS review; emergency path exists for security.

**Where docs will live (WIP but live links):**
• Confluence (Runtime LTS hub): https://confluence.redwoodinference.com/display/ENG/Runtime+LTS
• Branch + backport policy: https://confluence.redwoodinference.com/display/ENG/Runtime+LTS+Branch+and+Backport+Policy
• Cutting an LTS patch runbook: https://confluence.redwoodinference.com/display/ENG/Cutting+a+Runtime+LTS+Patch+Release
• LTS release notes format/taxonomy: https://confluence.redwoodinference.com/display/ENG/Runtime+LTS+Release+Notes+Format
• Known issues list (LTS): https://confluence.redwoodinference.com/display/ENG/Runtime+LTS+Known+Issues

**Heads up on expectations:**
• LTS prioritizes stability over feature parity. Some experimental runtime behavior will remain *off* on LTS even if it exists on mainline.
• If you’re landing large refactors in runtime: please assume “not backportable” unless we align up front.

We’ll post the pilot status + first RC timing in `#eng-releases`.

Mateo Alvarez: +1. This is a big enterprise unlock but we need to keep the bar high on perf + correctness. Please route customer pressure through the process vs ad-hoc cherry-picks. Thanks everyone.

Redwood Release Bot: :white_check_mark: Branch `runtime-1.18-lts` created and protected rules applied. Release CI workflow enabled for `runtime-1.18-lts/*`.

Talia Benson: Adding: we’re collecting feedback on the *slower-change* release notes template (what Support/CS needs to answer “should I upgrade?” quickly). If you’ve got examples of good/bad release notes from other vendors, DM me.

Rachel Kim: For CS: we have 2 pilot accounts tentatively lined up (Private + Dedicated). If your account is pushing hard on LTS, send me the details (esp change-control constraints + desired support window).

Dev Patel: Support folks — please don’t tell customers “we’ll backport it” until we’ve confirmed it qualifies. Use the intake template and we’ll turn around a decision fast.

Sean Gallagher: SRE note: LTS rollouts will have stricter monitoring + rollback readiness checks. If you’re oncall and see LTS traffic behaving oddly, tag `#eng-sre` + `#eng-releases` early — we’ll treat it like release-branch health.

Marissa Cole: Product side: we’re going to keep customer messaging simple — “stable baseline, predictable patches, slower-change notes.” If you hear confusion about “LTS == older/less secure,” flag it. Security patch path is part of the channel.

Hannah Schmitt: Question from Sales: what should we say about support window right now? Some prospects are asking for 12 months.

Connor O'Brien: @hannah for the pilot: we’re positioning `1.18.x-lts` as 6–12+ months *target* support window pending GA criteria. For any deal language, please route through @avery / @rachel / me so we don’t overcommit before we lock the final SLA.

Avery Johnson: +1. I’ll share the approved boilerplate in `#sales` once Legal/CS review it. For now: “monthly patches, security out-of-band, stability prioritized, pilot limited availability.”

Allison Grant: Perf gates reminder: we’re using p50/p95 + throughput thresholds on canonical workloads. Bench runners are a little noisy during peak CI, so if you see a flake, rerun before escalating.

Sahana Iyer: CI/build note: release branches require a slightly different toolchain image. If you see a release-branch-only failure, paste the job link in `#eng-infra` and tag me — we’re tightening it up this week.

Hiro Tanaka: Kernel note: we froze autotune defaults for LTS to reduce variance. Overrides still exist but please don’t recommend changing them for customers unless we’ve measured it.

Sofia Ivanova: Scheduling: we added targeted scheduler regression tests for backpressure + overload. If you’re touching queueing/backpressure code, please run the release-branch suite locally before asking for backport.

Elliot Price: Release notes folks: we’ll start publishing LTS notes in the Product docs area as well once the first patch is out. For now, Confluence is source of truth.

Rafael Mendes: Also: emergency security patches will have a tighter approval + comms checklist. If you’re pulled into one, assume it’s time-sensitive and we’ll prioritize getting you unblocked.

Connor O'Brien: Thanks all. Next update will be RC timing + pilot rollout plan in `#eng-releases` (and we’ll link it here once posted).

Talia Benson: :loudspeaker: Incident Bot v2 is now **GA** (enabled by default) in `#incidents` and `#eng-oncall`.

Big thanks to SRE + Platform + Telemetry for getting this over the line. The goal is consistent “first 5 minutes” context and lower MTTR.

**What’s new in v2 (high level):**
• **Recent deploys card**: service + version/image SHA, rollout phase (canary vs full), canary health, and links back to the PR/commit + deploy timeline.
• **Customer/tenant-safe config diffs**: feature flags, routing policy, quota/rate-limit, model pins. Includes **redaction** + “why redacted” indicators.
• **Saturation signals**: GPU util, queue depth, KV cache pressure, scheduler/backpressure indicators, and regional capacity summary.
• **Deep links**: 1-click links to the relevant dashboards/traces (pre-filled time window, service, region).
• **Degraded-mode behavior**: if upstream queries are slow/unhealthy, the bot falls back to cached context + links instead of hanging the thread.

**How to use it**
When an incident thread starts (or you run the bot command), you’ll see context cards posted near the top of the thread.
• If you need more detail on a card, use the interactive actions ("Expand diff", "Open dashboard", etc.).
• If you suspect sensitive info, please **do not paste raw configs**—use the bot diff card and request elevated access via the documented break-glass path.

**Where to file issues / feedback**
• Bugs / missing data / confusing cards: file a Linear ticket under Eng (tag `incident-bot`) or drop a thread in `#help` with a link to the incident thread.
• If you see redaction behaving oddly (too aggressive or too permissive): ping `@Naomi Feldman` / `@Marcus Lin` in-thread and file a ticket.

Rollout note: this is feature-flagged on the backend. If we see regressions (Slack spam, query load, missing context), SRE can roll it back quickly.

Rafael Mendes: :thumbsup: Adding: if a card is empty, please reply in the incident thread with the bot’s error line (it includes the request id). That’s the fastest way for us to trace query timeouts vs permissions vs missing mappings.

Miles Thornton: FYI for on-call: the v2 output is intentionally **summary-first**. Don’t wait for perfect data—use the links to jump into the dashboards while the bot fills in the rest.

Devon Price: If you hit Slack interaction weirdness (buttons spinning / “something went wrong”), try clicking again—actions are idempotent now. If it keeps failing, paste the “interaction_failed” line from the ephemeral message into `#help`.

Allison Grant: Saturation thresholds are standardized now. If the bot flags “HIGH” GPU/queue/KV, please treat it as a triage hint (not a page by itself). The runbook is updated with what-to-do-next for each signal.

Connor O'Brien: Deploy card is driven off the new deploy metadata export. If you see a service with “unknown rollout phase” or missing PR links, tag me + include the service name and approx deploy time window.

Naomi Feldman: Reminder: config diff cards are **tenant-safe** but still considered internal incident data. Please don’t screenshot/share outside approved channels. Redaction + access events are audited.

Marcus Lin: If you believe something sensitive leaked (even if it’s internal), please treat as a security incident and follow the standard reporting path + DM me. We have tests, but we’d rather overreact than miss something.

incident-bot: v2 diagnostics: enabled=true cohort=ga (request_id=diag_7f3a1b)  
Need help? Reply with `request_id` or file: https://linear.app/redwood/team/ENG (tag: incident-bot)
